question_text: Two identical shopping trolleys are being pushed across a supermarket floor. Trolley A experiences a forward push of 120 N and friction of 40 N. Trolley B experiences a forward push of 100 N and friction of 30 N. Which trolley has the greater resultant force, and by how much?
correct_answer: Trolley A has the greater resultant force. Trolley A: 120 − 40 = 80 N; Trolley B: 100 − 30 = 70 N. Difference = 80 − 70 = 10 N.
explanation: To find the resultant force, subtract the opposing friction force from the applied force. Trolley A's resultant is 80 N and Trolley B's is 70 N, so Trolley A has 10 N more resultant force. This means Trolley A will accelerate faster than Trolley B, even though it is being pushed with less force.
